hypothesis: If a chemically informed autoencoder latent space is used to condition a diffusion model on adsorption energy targets and elemental constraints, then it will generate shell-wise surface compositions that more accurately satisfy inverse design specifications (target adsorption energies and allowed element lists) compared to a chemically-agnostic baseline, because encoding periodic table relationships into the latent space captures the chemical similarity structure necessary for meaningful interpolation and conditional generation of catalytic surface compositions.
